question.text: Which of the following correctly describes the SCSS (Senior Citizens Savings Scheme) eligibility for a person who has taken voluntary retirement at age 56?

answer.text: Not eligible since the minimum age for SCSS is 60 years with no exceptions
Eligible since VRS retirees aged 55 and above can invest, but the account must be opened within one month of receiving retirement benefits
Eligible since VRS retirees aged 50 and above can invest at any time after retirement
Eligible but can invest only up to the amount received as retirement benefits, with no maximum cap

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SCSS allows early access for individuals aged 55+ who have retired under VRS (Voluntary Retirement Scheme) or superannuation, provided the account is opened within one month of receiving retirement benefits. The standard age is 60+. Defence personnel get an even lower threshold of 50+. The maximum investment limit is Rs. 30 lakh regardless of retirement benefits received.
**The Trap:** The "no exceptions" option ignores the VRS/superannuation/defence provisions. The age 50 option confuses the defence personnel exception with the VRS exception. The "no maximum cap" option ignores the Rs. 30 lakh limit. The one-month deadline for opening the account is a frequently missed detail.
**Cross-Exam Context:** SCSS eligibility conditions are tested in NISM X-A, X-B, and in banking certification exams. The one-month window for VRS retirees is a commonly tested time-bound condition.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** If a client is planning VRS, advise them to have SCSS documentation ready before receiving retirement benefits. The one-month window is strict and missing it means waiting until age 60. Also ensure the investment doesn't exceed Rs. 30 lakh.
